element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Choose between Shin Kazama, Mickey Simon, or Greg Gates with different plane capabilities
All pilots begin with $3000 and basic F8 Crusader; foundation for progression
Purchase special weapons and defenses; earned money from destroying enemies; unused weapons convert back to currency
Destroyed to earn money for shop; scaling difficulty through progression
Purchased upgrades that provide tactical variety; consumable per level
Continuous health pool consumed over single life; uncommon for arcade-style shooters; core survival mechanic

typical_tension_triggers: Health bar degradation from sustained damage
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based with progression-gated intensity; shop breaks provide rhythm reset between levels; energy bar creates constant low-level tension that escalates with damage accumulation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory combat with basic enemies; players learn energy bar mechanics and shop system
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy density and formation complexity; special weapon variety becomes tactical necessity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dense enemy patterns and likely boss encounters requiring accumulated upgrades
notable_spikes: First special weapon depletion without shop access
Transition from scattered enemies to organized formations (estimated mid-level)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level begins with optional shop visit for upgrades, creating natural pacing breaks and resource management decisions
Side-scrolling layout facilitates enemy waves emerging from right side; tension builds and releases with formation completion
Destroying enemies generates money; unspent weapons convert to currency; creates feedback loop where performance directly enables upgrades

sources_used: Wikipedia - U.N. Squadron (gameplay and mechanics sections)
